reasoning: The question asks whether Gukesh D's Elo rating will increase by at least 1% from its value on October 26 (assumed to be his October FIDE rating of 2794) by three resolution dates: Nov 2, Nov 25, and Jan 24. A 1% increase on 2794 is approximately 28 Elo points, requiring him to reach an Elo of ~2822. At the super-elite level, gaining 28 points in 1 to 3 months is exceedingly rare and difficult, requiring a performance significantly above expectation (roughly +3.0 expected score) against top players. Since the FIDE rankings update monthly, the November dates will rely on October's outcomes, where no such massive gain occurred. By the January date, even a dominant performance in the World Chess Championship against Ding Liren would mathematically struggle to yield 28 points due to the limited number of games and high rating of the opponent. Following the base rate of 0.007 for +1% Elo increases, the probabilities are capped at the minimum allowed bounds, with a slight adjustment for the longer forecast horizon.
